world_assessment: Quiet macro week with S&P near all-time highs. The standout portfolio event: Wells Fargo raised NVDA price target from $715 to $875, validating our highest-conviction semiconductor thesis. Fed's Rosengren flagged tight labor markets as persistent and raised stablecoin structural concerns — no direct portfolio impact, but confirms the 'higher for longer' rate environment that underpins our financial re-rating thesis. The critical problem remains the -6.8% gap vs S&P. With the market near ATH, the right move is portfolio quality consolidation — closing momentum-depleted positions and redeploying into cleaner setups with rising institutional trends.

thesis_updates.notes: Wells Fargo raised PT to $875, confirming institutional acceleration of the semiconductor thesis. ADX=41 (matching entry level), OBV rising, MACD bullish — trend is not decelerating. Per Lesson 9: price at $768 has exceeded the $650 original target but ADX is flat/rising from entry and OBV rising = raise target to $875. This is our best performer (+35.8%) and momentum signals demand we hold.

Price at $368.75 — within 2% of $375 target. ADX declined from ~38 at entry to 23 (15-point decline). MACD is bearish, OBV is falling. While the ADX decline doesn't technically breach Lesson 6's 20-point threshold, the combination of near-target price + OBV falling + MACD bearish constitutes a disciplined close. Closing at +18.2%.

ADX=20 and declining, OBV falling, MACD bearish. Only +6.9% on a position that should have benefited more from financial re-rating. Weakest of our three financial names. JPM provides equivalent theme exposure with cleaner fundamentals. Closing to free capital for better opportunities.

ADX declined from 37 to 26 (11-point decline), OBV has turned falling, MACD bearish. Still +42% above entry and well above the raised target. Per Lesson 6, need >20-point ADX decline before disciplined exit above target. At 11 points, not there yet — but on active watch. If ADX falls to 17 or OBV steepens lower, will close.

ADX=17, entry ADX was ~40 = 23-point decline. OBV flat (not falling). Price $398.87 vs $440 target = 9.3% below target (Lesson 7 requires 15%). Not triggering Lesson 7: ADX decline of 23 (not 30), ADX=17 (borderline but below 18), OBV flat. Healthcare Aging thesis structurally intact. Hold but monitoring closely.

RSI=39, below SMA50, OBV falling, MACD bearish. Technicals are weak but ADX=28 confirms some institutional presence. Rosengren's tight labor market comments support higher rate environment = expanding NII. Financial re-rating thesis intact. Hold.
RSI=41, below SMA50, OBV falling. Infrastructure bill not yet passed but politically viable. ADX=30 above threshold. +1.2% from entry. Thesis catalyst still ahead — hold.
OBV falling, MACD bearish. Only +1% from entry. EV transition thesis (F-150 Lightning, federal fleet mandates) intact. ADX=31 still confirms a trend. Hold with tight watch on OBV.

ADX=18 (declined meaningfully from entry), OBV rising, MACD bullish. Amazon/Microsoft/Google clean energy procurement news shows strategic alignment. Azure thesis intact. OBV rising offsets the ADX weakness. Hold.
ADX=24, OBV flat, MACD bearish. Gig economy driver apps article confirms Maps/platform dominance. No thesis change. Hold.
RSI=71, ADX=26, OBV rising, MACD bullish. Surgical robotics is a direct Healthcare Aging beneficiary as elective procedures normalize post-COVID. +15.1% gain. Hold.
ADX=35, OBV rising. GLP-1 franchise (Ozempic/Victoza) is a structural multi-decade growth story aligned with the aging/diabetes epidemic. Hold.

ADX=32, OBV rising. LNG export demand from Europe and Asia is a structural energy security thesis. Southwestern Energy emissions certification news suggests regulatory compliance becoming a moat for cleaner operators. Hold.
ADX=43 (strongest in portfolio), OBV rising. Slightly underwater (-0.2%) but institutional momentum is the most powerful signal we have. Diabetes/oncology pipeline is long-duration. Hold.

new_positions.thesis: DexCom is the structural leader in continuous glucose monitoring, a market with compounding secular tailwinds from the Healthcare Aging theme. As 10,000 Boomers turn 65 daily, Type 2 diabetes prevalence increases — CGM devices provide a recurring revenue model (sensor replacement every 10-14 days) that is non-discretionary healthcare spending. Beyond diagnosed diabetes, DexCom is expanding CGM into pre-diabetes and general metabolic health, dramatically extending the addressable market. The technology has clinical proof of reducing hospitalizations, making payer reimbursement expansion (not contraction) the structural trend. Biden's ACA expansion means more covered lives with CGM reimbursement. Unlike ISRG (procedure-dependent) or UNH (insurance exposure), DexCom is a pure medical device with recurring revenue — a different risk profile within Healthcare Aging. RSI=71, ADX=31, OBV rising, MACD bullish — all four signals aligned on a confirmed institutional trend.
new_positions.invalidation: ADX declines below 20 from entry with OBV falling simultaneously; or FDA holds G7 platform approval; or a major payer removes CGM reimbursement coverage
new_positions.timing_note: RSI=71, ADX=31, OBV rising, MACD bullish — clean institutional trend, not overextended

close_positions.reason: Price at $368.75 is 98% of the $375 target. ADX declined from ~38 at entry to 23, OBV is falling, MACD is bearish. Near-complete target achievement + OBV falling + MACD bearish = disciplined profit-taking at +18.2%. Financial re-rating thesis remains valid but momentum has transferred to JPM. Not letting a winner become a round-trip.

ADX=20 declining, OBV falling, MACD bearish. Only +6.9% gain on a position that should have benefited materially from the financial re-rating thesis. Weakest of our financial names with no upcoming catalyst to reignite momentum. JPM provides equivalent financial exposure with higher quality. Freeing capital for better-momentum opportunities.

lesson_updates.reason: NVDA provides the clearest possible validation this week: original target was $650, we raised it and held per Lesson 9. Now at $768 with Wells Fargo raising institutional PT to $875. ADX=41 (matching entry level) and OBV rising — the hold-above-target principle has generated an additional ~$1,300 in unrealized gains vs taking profits at $650. Incrementing to 5/5 — this lesson is now rock-solid and ready for belief absorption.

belief_updates.name: Thesis Integrity Over Price Action
belief_updates.description: When a position is in drawdown OR has exceeded its price target, the exit decision must be driven by institutional momentum signals — not price movement alone. Three-sided principle: (1) HOLD through drawdowns when ADX >25 with OBV rising and no fundamental thesis change — price weakness is noise, not signal; (2) HOLD and RAISE TARGET through target breaches when ADX is flat/rising from entry and OBV is rising — the market is confirming the thesis has more runway than originally modeled; (3) EXIT when: thesis breaks on fundamentals, OR ADX declines >20 points from entry AND MACD turns bearish AND price is at/above target (Lesson 6), OR ADX declines >30 points AND falls below 18 AND OBV falls pre-target (Lesson 7). The unifying principle: price alone is never the exit trigger — institutional momentum signals are. NVDA exemplifies principle #2: $650 target breached, ADX=41 stable from entry, Wells Fargo raising to $875.

weekly_summary: Week of Jun 24, 2021: Quiet macro week, S&P near ATH. NVDA surged to $768 (+35.8%) after Wells Fargo raised PT to $875 — target raised accordingly, Lesson 9 validated. Closed GS at +18.2% (near target, OBV failing) and BLK (weakest position, momentum gone) to sharpen portfolio quality. Opened DXCM at $425 as Healthcare Aging addition — CGM structural growth story with clean technicals (ADX=31, OBV rising). Portfolio now 13 positions at ~26% cash. LLY (ADX=43) and NVO (ADX=35) are the strongest healthcare holdings. NUE (+42%) on watch as OBV turns negative after 11-point ADX decline. Key lesson consolidated: Lesson 9 absorbed into core belief 'Thesis Integrity Over Price Action' after hitting 5/5 score.
